title: India’s Jio lays out sovereign LEO constellation plan ahead of IPO
summary: Jio Platforms plans to lease broadband capacity from satellite constellations to establish a sovereign LEO network in India ahead of its IPO. The move aims to bolster India's independent satellite broadband infrastructure.

title: MDA Space to buy Blue Canyon Technologies to gain foothold in U.S. market
summary: MDA Space is acquiring Blue Canyon Technologies for $620 million to strengthen its position in the U.S. government market. The deal provides MDA Space greater access to the lucrative U.S. space industry.

title: ElevationSpace Secures US $40 Million in Series B Funding, Bringing Total Raised to US $63.5 Million
summary: ElevationSpace raised $40 million in Series B funding, bringing its total funding to $63.5 million. The company focuses on space-to-Earth transportation and space environment utilization platforms.

title: Northrop Grumman says industry ready to scale solid rocket production, with longer contracts
summary: Northrop Grumman states that the space industry is prepared to increase solid rocket production, contingent on longer contracts. The company highlights the need for predictable demand from suppliers despite significant private investment.

title: The MEO durability crisis: why LEO hardware will fail the new orbital economy
summary: The article discusses the challenges of Medium Earth Orbit (MEO) durability compared to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) hardware, highlighting why LEO solutions may not suffice for the evolving orbital economy. It emphasizes the need for more robust infrastructure to support future space operations.

title: NASA selects mission to study space weather interaction with Earth’s atmosphere
summary: NASA has chosen a space science mission to investigate how space weather affects Earth's atmosphere. The mission aims to enhance understanding of this interaction.

title: Chinese university-led mission to study asteroid Apophis during close encounter with Earth
summary: A spacecraft developed by Tsinghua University will join international missions to study asteroid Apophis during its close approach to Earth in 2029. This mission aims to gather data on the asteroid's composition and behavior.

title: Mu-g Technologies enters the parabolic flight business
summary: Mu-g Technologies has acquired an aircraft to offer commercial parabolic flight services. This move comes as NASA seeks to acquire its own aircraft for reduced-gravity research.

title: Austrian propulsion startup joins sovereign space funding surge
summary: Austrian satellite propulsion startup Gate Space secured 6.3 million euros in funding from Europe's government-backed accelerator program, contributing to a broader push for space sovereignty in Europe.

title: EQT to acquire Exolaunch
summary: Private equity firm EQT is acquiring Exolaunch, a company specializing in satellite rideshare launches. The acquisition aims to address the increasing demand for launch services.

title: A private company will build and launch NASA's next Mars orbiter in 2028 — and it's not SpaceX
summary: A California-based private company will design, build, and launch NASA's next Mars orbiter in 2028 for the Aeolus mission, marking the first probe to provide daily measurements of the planet's global environment. This is not a SpaceX project.

title: 'This is not like life that we’ve experienced' 'Star City' cast on struggling to relate to the brutality of Soviet existence in Apple TV's For All Mankind' spin-off
summary: Apple TV+'s For All Mankind spin-off 'Star City' explores the harsh realities of Soviet life, with cast members struggling to relate to its brutality. The show contrasts this with their own experiences, emphasizing the stark differences in living conditions.

title: The most unpredictable meteor shower of the year peaks next week. Here's what to expect
summary: The June Bootids meteor shower, known for its unpredictability, is set to peak next week with potential surprise outbursts. Historically, it has produced fewer meteors but occasionally exhibits unexpected activity.

title: 'No one thought it was going to be possible.' A space telescope is falling out of space. This is NASA's daring plan to save it.
summary: NASA's Swift space observatory is falling out of orbit, and the agency is exploring a commercial solution to save it. A private company may attempt to build a spacecraft within nine months to rescue the telescope.

title: Menstruation in space will be studied for 1st time with 'Operation Period'
summary: Menstruation in space will be studied for the first time in a project called 'Operation Period', aiming to address challenges faced by female astronauts. The study could change how future space missions accommodate women's health needs.

title: Annie Easley, a hero of NASA | Space photo of the day for June 19, 2026
summary: Annie Easley, a pioneering NASA human computer, is featured in a space photo of the day for June 19, 2026. She contributed to the development of the Centaur upper-stage rocket.

title: Juicy new details emerge about an asteroid NASA's Lucy spacecraft flew by last year
summary: NASA's Lucy spacecraft discovered evidence of liquid water altering the asteroid Donaldjohanson, which originally formed farther from the sun before migrating inward.

title: Scientists trace high-energy ghost particle to the 'Shadow Blaster' galaxy
summary: Scientists have traced a high-energy ghost particle, a neutrino, to a galaxy dubbed 'Shadow Blaster,' marking a potential first direct link between an individual dusty star-forming galaxy and such an event.

title: James Webb Space Telescope finds a salty surprise on famous 'Pink Planet'
summary: Astronomers using the James Webb Space Telescope discovered that the exoplanet VHS 1256 b, nicknamed the 'Pink Planet,' has a salty atmosphere. This finding provides new insights into the composition of distant worlds.

title: Tropical Storm Arthur
summary: Tropical Storm Arthur marked the beginning of the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season, causing heavy rainfall and flash flood risks along the U.S. Gulf Coast.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4920-4926: Surveying the Bands
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover is surveying distinct rock layers on Mount Sharp, each with unique textures and tones, as part of its ongoing mission. The rover's activities are detailed in a blog post by a senior research scientist from the Space Science Institute.

title: NASA Mission to Study Space Weather Impacts of Earth’s Atmosphere
summary: NASA selected the DAPHNE mission to study space weather impacts on Earth's atmosphere and improve predictions for technology like GPS and satellites. The mission advances to Phase B of development.

title: NASA Awards Contract for Commercial Satellite Data Acquisition
summary: NASA has selected eight new companies and extended contracts with six existing ones to acquire commercial satellite data, expanding research and decision-making resources. The data will supplement NASA's Earth satellites with high-resolution and frequent observations.

title: From Suriname to Space: Rohit Goeptar Shares His Journey to NASA
summary: Rohit Goeptar shared his inspiring journey from a poor family in Suriname to working at NASA. His family's move to California and eventual return to South America shaped his path to space exploration.

title: Desert Field Test With NASA Advanced Rover Prototype
summary: NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory tested a prototype rover named ERNEST in the Colorado Desert in March 2026. The rover, designed for advanced mobility and robotic autonomy, served as a testbed for new autonomy software.

title: NASA Testing Advanced Capabilities for Moon, Mars Rovers
summary: NASA is testing the ERNEST rover in the Colorado Desert to advance robotic autonomy and navigation for future Moon and Mars missions. The prototype recently traveled 16 miles with minimal human intervention.

title: NASA’s Lucy Reveals Wobbling, Peanut-Shaped Asteroid
summary: NASA's Lucy spacecraft discovered that the asteroid Donaldjohanson is wobbly and peanut-shaped, formed from fragments after a collision 155 million years ago. The asteroid's complex history suggests significant activity despite its small size.

title: Stages of Star Formation
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope captured an image showing various stages of star formation in the Orion Molecular Cloud. The image highlights stellar embryos and protoplanetary discs within a cold gas and dust filament.

title: Hubble Glimpses Merging Galaxy Clusters
summary: The NASA Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of a merging galaxy cluster, CL0016+1609 or MACS J0018.5+1626, which is bright in X-ray wavelengths and has been extensively studied. The cluster's X-ray observations revealed it is composed of two merging clusters along the line of sight.

title: Canadarm2 repairs planned as CRS-34 departs ISS
summary: The Expedition 74 crew on the ISS is planning repairs for the Canadarm2 as the CRS-34 mission departs. This marks another busy month of operations aboard the station.

title: Terran R second stage heads to Stennis as Relativity advances toward 2026 debut
summary: Relativity Space is advancing toward the 2026 debut of its Terran R heavy-lift rocket, with the second stage now transported to Stennis Space Center for testing.

title: Launch Preview: 10 launches scheduled from sites across the world
summary: Ten launches are scheduled worldwide in the upcoming week, marking a busy period for the space industry. The launches will take place from various sites across the globe.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket after weeks of delays
summary: Isar Aerospace canceled the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket after multiple delays. The European private space launch provider is headquartered in Ottobrunn, Germany.

title: Booster 20 Completes Cryo, As Ship 40 Nears Static Fire
summary: Booster 20 has completed cryogenic testing, while Ship 40 is nearing a static fire test following the recent Flight 12. The focus is now on these next milestones in the development program.

title: China successfully debuts tallest rocket, LandSpace prepares for second landing attempt
summary: China successfully launched its tallest rocket, while LandSpace is preparing for a second attempt at a rocket landing. The missions are part of a busy month for China's space activities.

title: Japan’s H3 to returns to flight with debut launch of lightest configuration
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight with its debut launch of the lightest configuration. The mission marks a significant milestone for Japan's space program after a previous failure.

title: Stoke Space completes Nova Stage 1 structural verification
summary: Stoke Space has completed the proto-qualification structural verification of the first stage of its Nova rocket. This milestone marks progress in the development of the company's reusable launch vehicle.

title: Artemis III Crew Named: Veteran Astronauts to test Lunar Landers in LEO
summary: NASA has named the four astronauts who will fly the Artemis III mission to test lunar landers in low Earth orbit (LEO). These veteran astronauts will prepare for the upcoming crewed lunar landing mission.

title: Booster 20 into Cryogenic Testing ahead of Starship Flight 13
summary: SpaceX's Starbase team has begun cryogenic testing on Booster 20 in preparation for the upcoming Starship Flight 13. This follows the recent conclusion of Starship Flight 12.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of the European Space Agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm designed for space applications, highlighting its versatility and potential impact on future missions. The innovation is expected to streamline operations and reduce costs in the space industry.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme enables European research institutes to participate in space science missions. The latest success is the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission launch in Norway.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are testing materials for the European Space Agency to ensure they can withstand the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is driving innovation with fresh perspectives.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) to enhance training, operations, and simulations, while also engaging the public with immersive space experiences.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical test for spacecraft parachutes, known as a 'bake-out,' to ensure their reliability during missions. This process involves heating the parachutes to remove moisture and contaminants.

title: Boeing Completes Quantum Lab Test Ahead of 2027 Flight
summary: Boeing successfully demonstrated high-fidelity quantum entanglement swapping during ground tests of its quantum networking satellite, Q4S. The test is a precursor to a planned 2027 flight.

title: True Anomaly Declares Mission X-3 Success
summary: True Anomaly announced the successful completion of Mission X-3 with its autonomous orbital vehicle, Jackal, achieving all test objectives and being fully commissioned.

title: Instinct Space Unveils Plans for Low-Cost Lunar Landers
summary: Instinct Space has shifted its focus from navigation support for lunar missions to developing low-cost lunar landers. The company aims to directly participate in lunar surface missions.

title: Katalyst Raises $12M to Extend Satellite Servicing to GEO
summary: Katalyst Space Technologies raised $12 million to expand its satellite servicing capabilities to geostationary orbit (GEO). The company plans its first GEO mission in 2027, following a near-term mission launching this month.

title: Dawn Aerospace Raises $25M Series B
summary: Dawn Aerospace has raised $25 million in a Series B funding round to accelerate the development of its Aurora spaceplane and conduct an in-space refueling demonstration.

title: Bill Spotlight: NEW HORIZON Act
summary: A bipartisan bill called the NEW HORIZON Act has been introduced to allow national security missions to utilize commercial orbital data centers. The bill aims to leverage the growing trend of commercial space-based data infrastructure.

title: Optera Raises £3M to Open New UK HQ
summary: Optera raised £3 million to establish a new UK headquarters, aligning with the UK government's push to enhance sovereign technical capabilities.

title: PiLogic Partners with AFRL on AI Anomaly Detection Tech
summary: PiLogic has partnered with AFRL to develop AI anomaly detection technology, currently testing it on terrestrial flat sats with plans to expand into orbit.

title: Space Stocks Dip After SpaceX IPO
summary: Space stocks declined following SpaceX's successful IPO, with major space companies experiencing a pullback. This marked a contrast to SpaceX's strong market debut.

title: JAXA Nails H3 Return to Flight
summary: JAXA successfully launched the H3 rocket on its eighth mission, marking its first successful flight with a new three-engine variant. The rocket delivered all six of its payloads to space from Japan's Tanegashima Space Center.

title: Quantum Space to Demonstrate Orbital Refueling Under Pentagon Contract
summary: Quantum Space will demonstrate orbital refueling using its Ranger spacecraft under a Pentagon contract awarded on June 18. The company aims to showcase in-space servicing capabilities for national security applications.

title: Iceye Data to Support Flood Monitoring in Norway
summary: The Norwegian Water Resources and Energy Directorate (NVE) has partnered with Iceye to use its satellite data for flood monitoring. This collaboration aims to enhance flood prediction and management in Norway using advanced satellite capabilities.

title: Vantor and Rheinmetall Plan Joint Venture for Sovereign Space in Germany
summary: Vantor and Rheinmetall are forming a joint venture to develop sovereign space capabilities in Germany and Europe. The collaboration was announced through a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU).

title: Private Equity Firm EQT to Acquire Rideshare Provider Exolaunch
summary: Private equity firm EQT is acquiring rideshare provider Exolaunch, marking EQT's first investment in the space sector. The acquisition was announced on June 18 by Stockholm-based EQT.

title: Intellian Calls New Production Facility in California a ‘Defining Milestone’
summary: Intellian Technologies is opening a new large-scale manufacturing facility in California, marking a significant milestone for the company. CEO Eric Sung described it as a defining moment for the firm.

title: Lyntris Acquires Robotics Firm Novium to Further Satellite Offerings
summary: Lyntris, a newly formed defense tech company, acquired Houston-based robotics firm Novium to expand its satellite capabilities. The acquisition follows Lyntris's recent formation through the merger of Vitesse.

title: Bezos Earth Fund Invests in FireSat Constellation Build-Out
summary: The Bezos Earth Fund is investing $26 million to support the development of the FireSat constellation, a wildfire-monitoring satellite system. This initiative aims to enhance wildfire detection and response capabilities.

title: Thursday Morning Conversation With Tina Ghataore
summary: The article features an interview with Tina Ghataore, a former Satellite Executive of the Year, discussing future developments in the satellite industry. It also mentions some breaking news related to the space sector.

title: Gitai Delays Robotic Servicing Mission to Prioritize SBI Work
summary: Gitai has completed a flight model for a robotic on-orbit servicing mission but is delaying the launch to prioritize space-based interceptor (SBI) technologies following recent U.S. Space developments.

title: DHS Promotes Latest Space Cybersecurity Research in SPARTA
summary: The Department of Homeland Security's Science and Technology Directorate has unveiled new space cybersecurity research under the Aerospace SPARTA framework to safeguard critical infrastructure. This initiative aims to enhance protections for space-based assets and systems.
